query_normalized: the impact of bounded confidence on opinion polarization speed psychology
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) as it investigates the mechanism of interpersonal influence and opinion dynamics (specifically intolerance and susceptibility) within a psychological agent-based model, which is the core domain of the user's question regarding bounded confidence and polarization. Additionally, it meets criterion (c) by establishing an empirical baseline for how psychological realism in opinion models reproduces real-world polarization data, providing the necessary foundational
